headline: Goldman Sachs Retirement Survey 2025: 58% of Americans Expect to Outlive Their Savings, and the Income Strategy That Changes the Math
summary: The Goldman Sachs Retirement Survey 2025 captures a tension that runs through the entire retirement landscape. A full 68% of working respondents say they are confident they will meet their retirement goals. At the same time, 58% believe they will outlive their savings. Goldman calls this the Optimism Gap, a split between how workers feel ... Goldman Sachs Retirement Survey 2025: 58% of Americans Expect to Outlive Their Savings, and the Income Strategy That Changes the Math

headline: How The Investment Story Is Shifting For Verizon Communications (VZ)
summary: Verizon Communications now carries an assessed fair value price target of US$51.85 per share, compared with the prior US$51.58. That shift sits against mixed analyst research, where some firms have raised targets by US$1 to US$5 and one major broker now uses US$56, while others question the growth outlook and disclosure quality. As you read on, you will see how to interpret these moves and follow the evolving narrative around Verizon. Stay updated as the Fair Value for Verizon Communications...

headline: A $185,000 Portfolio That Covers Your Groceries, Utilities, and Phone Bill Every Month
summary: The average American household spends about $519 a month on groceries, based on the latest BLS Consumer Expenditure Survey figure of $6,224 a year, and grocery prices are still rising: food-at-home prices were up 1.9% year over year as of March 2026. Add about $280 for utilities and roughly $100 for phone and internet, plus ... A $185,000 Portfolio That Covers Your Groceries, Utilities, and Phone Bill Every Month

headline: Verizon: The Buy Case Still Holds After Q1
summary: Verizon recently released its Q1 '26 earnings, and the share price was up 1.6% following EPS beating expectations. See why I rate VZ stock a Buy.
